scene1_hook: Breaking tonight — the skincare industry may never recover.
scene2_problem: For decades, women have spent billions on creams, serums, and dermatologist visits. Experts now say most of it was overpriced. Significantly overpriced.

scene2_overlays.text: Americans Spent $22 Billion on Skincare Last Year

scene3_stakes: A clinical-grade Vitamin C compound — used in dermatology offices for years — is now available direct to consumer. At a fraction of the cost. Dermatologists in three states have filed formal complaints.
scene4_solution: The product is called LumaGlow. Twenty percent pure Vitamin C. Clinically tested. Results reported in as little as seven days. We reached out to the manufacturer for comment. They said: it works.

scene4_overlays.text: LumaGlow: 20% Vitamin C  |  Results in 7 Days  |  30-Day Guarantee

scene5_ask: Try LumaGlow risk-free for thirty days. If your skin doesn't glow, you pay nothing. Order at LumaGlow dot com. This has been Freddy Scott. Good night.
tagline: This has been Freddy Scott. Good night.
